title: Review: five-round review of all backends
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one new P0 bug (aws-ssm put error mapping), one UX footgun that will generate user reports (GCP token expiry), two test infrastructure issues. No security-surface changes needed.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: azure-kv and bitwarden backends not read in detail this pass (they were fixed in earlier rounds but could have the same put() error-mapping gap as aws-ssm). Would raise confidence if: read azure-kv and bitwarden put() error handling; run cargo test --all-features with full integration env. The aws-ssm put() P0 is new — it was not caught in 4 previous rounds, which means the remaining unread backends may have the same class of bug.
